task: An aerospace propulsion program found a thermal-shield support bracket outside drawing tolerance after engine hot-fire acceptance testing. The material-review board needs a computational disposition pack: use-as-is, repair, rework, or scrap, with fatigue and thermal-margin evidence. removed context: FAA/EASA continued-airworthiness and production-conformity concepts, AS9100 nonconforming-output control practices, NASA/industry thermal-structural analysis methods, and public metallic fatigue allowables references reviewed June 2026. Embedded packet: part=exhaust thermal shield bracket BRK-77, material=Inconel 718 aged, drawing thickness=2.40 mm +/-0.10, measured thickness grid mm=[2.31,2.28,2.25,2.22,2.19,2.24,2.29,2.33], hole_edge_distance spec>=5.00 mm measured=[5.12,5.08,4.86,5.02], flatness spec<=0.35 mm measured=0.62, hot-fire exposure=3 cycles, peak metal temperature thermocouples C=[612,628,641,637], design_limit_temp=650 C, normal operating cycles remaining=18,000, stress_model_nominal MPa at 2.40mm=382, stress scales inversely with thickness, local Kt for edge_distance<5.0 =1.08, flatness bending add=22 MPa if flatness>0.50, material allowable yield at 650C=860 MPa, fatigue allowable alternating stress for 20k cycles at 640C=510 MPa with scatter_factor=1.35, inspection finding: no crack by fluorescent penetrant, oxide discoloration moderate, repair option R1 blend hole edge cost=$9,800 downtime=2 days removes Kt but consumes 0.04 mm local thickness, R2 re-form flatness cost=$18,500 downtime=4 days reduces flatness to 0.28 but requires repeat heat tint inspection, R3 replacement cost=$74,000 lead_time=21 days, program slip cost=$46,000/day. Rules and constraints: compute minimum-thickness stress=382*(2.40/min_thickness); apply Kt and flatness add where applicable; fatigue_margin = fatigue_allowable/scatter_factor - computed_stress; use-as-is allowed only if temperature margin>=9 C, yield margin>=1.5, fatigue_margin>=25 MPa, all dimensional nonconformances have risk rationale, and no crack indication; repair plan must include post-repair stress effect; choose disposition minimizing cost+slip while satisfying margins; flag expert-review items separately from calculations. Required work: deliver thermal_structural_mrb_analysis.xlsx + nonconformance_disposition_memo.md with stress/fatigue calculations, dimensional map, repair economics, MRB disposition recommendation, required inspections, and airworthiness-style rationale based only on the packet.
